description_long: This is a tall, slender ceramic vase with a single curved handle attached to its neck and shoulder. The body is decorated with black-figure artwork, depicting human figures in classical attire, likely inspired by ancient Greek pottery. The neck is narrow and flares outward at the rim, while the base is small and rounded, providing a stable footing. The surface shows a matte, slightly rough finish with visible wear and patina, suggesting age or intentional antiquing. The color palette consists mainly of earthy terracotta and black, with the painted motifs standing out against the lighter background. The handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the form, enhancing both function and aesthetics.
description: A tall, slender terracotta vase with a single handle and black-figure artwork depicting classical human figures. The surface is matte and slightly worn, with earthy tones and a flared rim.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main black-figure artwork with two human figures and the handle on the right.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the plain terracotta surface with minimal decoration and the handle centered.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the handle on the left and a partial view of the painted figures.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional black-figure artwork with two more human figures in motion.
